behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: evaluate-stage writer -> artifact store slot selection. A unit test of a pure helper would miss wiring bugs where the wrong artifact key is passed. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model judgment. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about destination sloting ('stores grounding ... not `plan`'), which is deterministic routing logic reproducible with in-process store fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command against an in-process artifact repository in a run context; assert one write to `grounding`, zero unintended overwrite of existing `plan`, and preserved metadata (run/issue scope).

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration (artifact read path + implement write path). The real failure is incorrect wiring between artifact lookup and output persistence, not single-function logic. No real infra required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text constrains storage contract ('reads grounding ... writes ... to `plan`'), not quality of model-generated content; deterministic stub implementation output is sufficient to expose the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic model stub, verify grounding is read (e.g., passed into builder context) and resulting artifact is written to `plan` only.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at interaction between deep-dive decision logic and artifact persistence side effects. Unit logic test alone can miss accidental write calls in wiring. No OS/network/subprocess dependency. Rejected higher levels: text specifies side-effect suppression based on existing issue diagnosis, which is deterministic branch behavior and can be validated with in-process fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with instrumented in-process artifact store, assert no write/update call to `plan` while expected non-plan outputs still occur.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: This is a state-handoff bug across local modules (run context scoping, artifact lineage, implement retrieval). It requires module wiring, not real OS/network. Rejected Agentic/Workflow despite multi-stage context: behavior is lineage/addressability ('read artifact produced earlier in same run context'), independent of model judgment quality; deterministic staged fixtures reproduce failures.
behaviors.test_description: Within one synthetic run context, write grounding from evaluate stage, then invoke implement retrieval path; assert implement resolves the exact prior artifact version/id and does not cross-read from another run/issue context.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end multi-step agent pipeline and semantic carryover across stages. Real failure can emerge only after sequential agentic decisions (grounding generation/use in later step). Mocking all model behavior would hide whether grounding actually influences final plan. Rejected lower levels: text requires 'full multi-step agent run' and final plan to 'reflect grounding', which is not fully validated by isolated integration wiring tests.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real staged pipeline in one run context with grounding-bearing input; assert the final implement plan includes/uses key grounding constraints from earlier stage and remains run-linked across deep-dive, evaluate, and implement steps.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback orchestration and artifact integrity under missing optional input. The failure boundary is module interaction (artifact lookup miss + fallback path + persistence), not OS/network or model quality. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is safety/integrity under absence ('grounding is absent ... still emits valid plan ... without corrupting other artifacts'), which is deterministic and reproducible with in-process stores/stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement flow in context with no grounding artifact and preexisting unrelated artifacts; assert successful `plan` emission, no mutation of unrelated artifact records, and expected fallback markers/flags.
